Off Calendar May-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Athina Kotsia, Esq. (no information) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

The court notes there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule and does not hear oral argument.

Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALFRED ANGUIANO, BEN HALL, and ZYNGA INC.'s Motion For Protective Order Re Deposition Of Yaron Leyvand:

The motion is granted because Plaintiff has not demonstrated good cause that "that the official [whose deposition Plaintiff sought to take] has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information" as required by Liberty Mutual Insurance Company et al v. Superior Court, 10 Cal. App. 4th 1282, 1289 (1992).

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
